target.title: refactor(qa): reuse transport capabilities in scenario runtime
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

QA Lab already routes qa-channel and Crabline suite runs through the same `QaTransportAdapter` capability surface. The scenario runtime API had a separate partial capability shape, which made it easier to introduce another parallel channel-driver abstraction instead of reusing the existing transport contract.

## Why This Change Was Made

This makes the existing transport capability contract explicit by exporting `QaTransportCapabilities` from `qa-transport.ts` and typing the scenario runtime environment directly against it. It intentionally does not add a new scenario-driver interface or change current routing behavior.

## User Impact

No end-user behavior changes. QA developers get a clearer foundation for follow-up portable channel scenarios: use the existing transport adapter capabilities that already back qa-channel and Crabline runs.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-qa.config.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-runtime-api.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/qa-channel-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean on the reduced diff
